---
title: "Additional visuals not already covered"
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    number_sections: true
    latex_engine: xelatex
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)

library(tidyverse)
library(kableExtra)

##ibm colorblind palette
ibm_blue    <- "#648FFF"
ibm_purple  <- "#785EF0"
ibm_magenta <- "#DC267F"
ibm_orange  <- "#FE6100"
ibm_gold    <- "#FFB000"

##styling
theme_ibm <- function() {
  theme_light() +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 9, color = "gray40"),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "#E8EEFF", color = NA),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10, color = "#1a1a1a"),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 8),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90")
  )
}

kable_style <- function(kbl) {
  kbl |>
    kableExtra::kable_styling(
      latex_options = c("striped", "hold_position"),
      stripe_color  = "#f5f5f5",
      font_size      = 10
    )
}

subtype_levels <- c("Theft", "Vehicle Theft", "Vandalism",
                    "Burglary", "Shoplifting",
                    "Assault", "Robbery", "Sexual Offenses", "Other Violent")
```

\newpage

# Cross Model Comparisons

## Crime Type

```{r type}
##f1 and mcc comparison across all models - replace values with actual results
model_metrics <- tibble(
  model  = factor(rep(c("Naive Bayes", "Elastic Net", "Random Forest", "XGBoost"),
                      each = 2),
                  levels = c("Naive Bayes", "Elastic Net", "Random Forest", "XGBoost")),
  metric = rep(c("F1 Score", "MCC"), times = 4),
  value  = c(
    0.362, 0.148,  ## naive bayes
    0.366, 0.154,  ## elastic net
    0.375, 0.173,  ## random forest
    0.833, 0.233   ## xgboost
  )
)

ggplot(model_metrics, aes(x = model, y = value, fill = metric)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.65), width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", value)),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.65),
    vjust = -0.4, size = 3
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("F1 Score" = ibm_blue, "MCC" = ibm_magenta),
    name   = "Metric"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +
  labs(
    title = "Crime Type Model Performance: F1 Score and MCC",
    x     = "Model",
    y     = "Score"
  ) +
  theme_ibm() +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
```

## Crime Subtype

```{r subtype}
##f1 and mcc comparison across all models - replace values with actual results
model_metrics <- tibble(
  model  = factor(rep(c("Naive Bayes", "Elastic Net", "Random Forest", "XGBoost"),
                      each = 2),
                  levels = c("Naive Bayes", "Elastic Net", "Random Forest", "XGBoost")),
  metric = rep(c("F1 Score", "MCC"), times = 4),
  value  = c(
    0.228, 0.089,  ## naive bayes
    0.105, 0.042,  ## elastic net
    0.156, 0.094,  ## random forest
    0.233, 0.121   ## xgboost
  )
)

ggplot(model_metrics, aes(x = model, y = value, fill = metric)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.65), width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = round(value, 3)),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.65),
    vjust = -0.4, size = 3
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("F1 Score" = ibm_blue, "MCC" = ibm_magenta),
    name   = "Metric"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +
  labs(
    title = "Crime Subtype Model Performance: F1 Score and MCC",
    x     = "Model",
    y     = "Score"
  ) +
  theme_ibm() +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
```

# Naive Bayes

## Equations

$$P(\text{Violent} \mid \mathbf{x}) \propto P(\text{Violent}) \prod_{j \in S} P(x_j \mid \text{Violent})$$

\newpage

# Elastic Net

## Tables

```{r en-metrics-table}
en_metrics <- tibble(
  Metric = c(
    "F1 Score", "MCC",
    "Accuracy", "AUC",
    "Sensitivity", "Specificity", 
    "Precision", "Balanced Accuracy"
  ),
  Value = c(
    "0.366", "0.154",
    "0.560", "0.627",
    "0.659", "0.536", 
    "0.253", "0.598"
  )
)

en_metrics |>
  kbl(
    booktabs  = TRUE,
    align     = c("l", "r"),
    col.names = c("Metric", "Value"),
    caption   = "Elastic Net: Crime Type Classification Metrics"
  ) |>
  kable_style() |>
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) |>
  pack_rows("Primary Metrics", 1,  2) |>
  pack_rows("Overall",         3,  8)
```

```{r en-coef-table}
en_coefs <- tibble(
  Predictor = c(
    "College Degree Rate", "Single Parent Rate", "No Vehicle Rate",
    "No HS Diploma Rate", "Disability Rate", "Cluster: Low Disadvantage",
    "Internet Access Rate", "Poverty Rate", "Cluster: Highest Disadvantage"
  ),
  Coefficient = c(
    -0.820,  0.713,  0.629,
     0.546,  0.208, -0.101,
    -0.092,  0.065,  0.058
  )
)

en_coefs |>
  arrange(desc(abs(Coefficient))) |>
  kbl(
    booktabs  = TRUE,
    digits    = 4,
    align     = c("l", "r"),
    col.names = c("Predictor", "Coefficient"),
    caption   = "Non-Zero Elastic Net Coefficients"
  ) |>
  kable_style() |>
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)
```

\newpage

## F1 Graph

```{r en-subtype-f1-plot, fig.height=4, fig.width=6.5, fig.align="center"}
## Replace values with exact per-class F1s from your model output
en_f1_per_class <- c(
  "Theft"           = 0.114,
  "Vehicle Theft"   = 0.168,
  "Vandalism"       = 0.043,
  "Burglary"        = 0.192,
  "Shoplifting"     = 0.170,
  "Assault"         = 0.117,
  "Robbery"         = 0.096,
  "Sexual Offenses" = 0.021,
  "Other Violent"   = 0.027
)

en_macro_f1 <- 0.105
en_f1_type  <- 0.366

tibble(
  Subtype = factor(subtype_levels, levels = rev(subtype_levels)),
  Type    = c(rep("Property", 5), rep("Violent", 4)),
  F1      = en_f1_per_class[subtype_levels]
) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Subtype, y = F1, fill = Type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = en_macro_f1,
             linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = en_f1_type,
             linetype = "dashed", color = ibm_blue, linewidth = 0.5) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.6, y = en_macro_f1,
           label = paste0(" Macro F1 = ", round(en_macro_f1, 3)),
           vjust = -0.4, hjust = 0, size = 2.8, color = "gray40") +
  annotate("text", x = 3, y = en_f1_type,
           label = paste0("Crime Type F1 = ", round(en_f1_type, 3)),
           vjust = -0.4, hjust = 1.1, size = 2.8, color = ibm_blue) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Property" = ibm_purple, "Violent" = ibm_magenta)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.5)) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title    = "Elastic Net Per-Subtype F1 Score",
       subtitle = "Gray dashed = subtype macro F1; blue dashed = crime type F1",
       x = NULL, y = "F1 Score", fill = NULL) +
  theme_ibm() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

## Equations

$$L(\boldsymbol{\beta}) = -\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^{n}\left[y_i \log(\hat{p}_i) + (1 - y_i)\log(1 - \hat{p}_i)\right] + \lambda\left[\alpha\sum_{j=1}^{p}|\beta_j| + \frac{1-\alpha}{2}\sum_{j=1}^{p}\beta_j^2\right]$$

# Random Forest

## Equations

$$\hat{y}_i = \mathbb{1}\left(\frac{1}{B}\sum_{b=1}^{B} T_b(\mathbf{x}_i) > \tau\right)$$

# XGBoost 

## Confusion Matrix

```{r xgb-cm-type, fig.height=4.5, fig.width=5, fig.align="center"}
xgb_cm <- tibble(
  Predicted  = factor(
    c("Violent", "Violent",  "Property", "Property"),
    levels = c("Property", "Violent")
  ),
  Actual     = factor(
    c("Violent", "Property", "Violent",  "Property"),
    levels = c("Violent", "Property")
  ),
  n          = c(10170, 21330, 18712, 99632),
  pct        = c(10170, 21330, 18712, 99632) / (10170 + 21330 + 18712 + 99632),
  cell_type  = c("tp", "fp", "fn", "tn"),
  cell_label = c("True Positive", "False Positive", "False Negative", "True Negative")
)

xgb_cm |>
  mutate(display = paste0(
    format(n, big.mark = ","), "\n",
    "(", sprintf("%.1f%%", pct * 100), ")\n",
    cell_label
  )) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted, fill = cell_type)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = display), fontface = "bold", size = 3.2, lineheight = 1.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      tp = "#F4E2F8",   ## light magenta – True Positive  (correct, low concentration)
      tn = ibm_magenta, ## solid magenta – True Negative  (correct, high concentration)
      fp = ibm_blue,    ## solid blue    – False Positive (incorrect, higher count)
      fn = "#E3EBFF"    ## light blue    – False Negative (incorrect, lower count)
    ),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Confusion Matrix: Crime Type",
    subtitle = "threshold = 0.5",
    x        = "Actual Class",
    y        = "Predicted Class"
  ) +
  theme_ibm() +
  theme(
    panel.grid   = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks   = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    axis.text    = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold")
  )
```

## F1 Graph

```{r xgb-subtype-f1-plot, fig.height=4, fig.width=6.5, fig.align="center"}
xgb_f1_per_class <- c(
  "Theft"           = 0.280,
  "Vehicle Theft"   = 0.247,
  "Vandalism"       = 0.139,
  "Burglary"        = 0.223,
  "Shoplifting"     = 0.398,
  "Assault"         = 0.259,
  "Robbery"         = 0.018,
  "Sexual Offenses" = 0.000,
  "Other Violent"   = 0.000
)

xgb_macro_f1 <- 0.233
xgb_f1_type  <- 0.833                   

tibble(
  Subtype = factor(subtype_levels, levels = rev(subtype_levels)),
  Type    = c(rep("Property", 5), rep("Violent", 4)),
  F1      = xgb_f1_per_class[subtype_levels]
) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Subtype, y = F1, fill = Type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = xgb_macro_f1,
             linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = xgb_f1_type,
             linetype = "dashed", color = ibm_blue, linewidth = 0.5) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.6, y = xgb_macro_f1,
           label = paste0(" Macro F1 = ", round(xgb_macro_f1, 3)),
           vjust = -0.4, hjust = 0, size = 2.8, color = "gray40") +
  annotate("text", x = 3, y = xgb_f1_type,
           label = paste0("Crime Type F1 = ", round(xgb_f1_type, 3)),
           vjust = -0.4, hjust = 1.1, size = 2.8, color = ibm_blue) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Property" = ibm_purple, "Violent" = ibm_magenta)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title    = "XGBoost Per-Subtype F1 Score",
       subtitle = "Gray dashed = subtype macro F1; blue dashed = crime type F1",
       x = NULL, y = "F1 Score", fill = NULL) +
  theme_ibm() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

# RQ2 

## Equations 

$$y_i = \beta_0 + \sum_{k=1}^{p} \beta_k x_{ik} + \varepsilon_i$$

$$\hat{y}_i = 1.724 - 0.159(\text{Median Income}) + 0.235(\text{No Vehicle Rate}) + 
0.088(\text{No HS Diploma Rate}) + 0.053(\text{Single Parent Rate})$$

$$y_i = \beta_0(u_i, v_i) + \sum_{k=1}^{p} \beta_k(u_i, v_i)\, x_{ik} + \varepsilon_i$$

$$\hat{\boldsymbol{\beta}}(u_i, v_i) = \left(\mathbf{X}'\mathbf{W}(u_i, v_i)\mathbf{X}\right)^{-1} 
\mathbf{X}'\mathbf{W}(u_i, v_i)\mathbf{y}$$

$$y_i = \beta_0(u_i, v_i) + \sum_{k=1}^{p} \beta_{h_k, k}(u_i, v_i)\, x_{ik} + \varepsilon_i$$
